QF.id: VIII Introduction to Clearing and Settlement System_37

question.text: The market-wide position limit (MWPL) for individual stock derivatives is set at:

answer.text: 20% of the free-float market capitalization expressed in number of shares

explanation.text:
**The Logic:** For individual stock derivatives, SEBI sets the MWPL at 20% of the number of shares held by non-promoters (free-float). Once the aggregate open interest across all exchanges reaches 95% of MWPL, no new positions can be created. Only offsetting (closing) positions are allowed.
**The Trap:** It is 20% of free-float (non-promoter holding), not total outstanding shares or market cap in rupee terms.
**Cross-Exam Context:** MWPL limits and the 95% threshold are important regulatory concepts.
**Key Takeaway:** Stock MWPL = 20% of free-float shares. At 95% of MWPL, only closing positions allowed. Prevents excessive speculation.
